kimberly-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
kimberly-stand-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK or into Bushin Prism Strikes; Run~Stop cancel: -8/-15 (OD: -5/-12)

kimberly-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
kimberly-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: Important combo tool with a Target Combo follow-up; Run~Stop cancel: +1/-4 (OD: +4/-1); collision box extends forward, allowing Kimberly to set up a tricky auto-shimmy with whiffed 5MP on a knocked down opponent; cancel into Run~Stop leaves Kim at +0 oB vs. Burnout (against reversal Super, sets up timing to kara cancel her next attack into Parry/Super)

kimberly-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
notes: Add +23 frames of advantage on Punish Counter
kimberly-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: In punishes, can cancel to Run~Stop or Teleport to maintain close position, then link into 5HK or 5HP; single hit confirmable, but will not combo to 236HP on the final cancel frame; Run~Stop cancel: +3/-4 (OD: +6/-1); can frame trap into meterless Arc Step; edge of hitbox is raised to make the attack more vulnerable to low-profile attacks; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

kimberly-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
kimberly-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Good range for a cancelable light normal; possible to chain 4 lights (2LP, 2LP, 5LP, 5LK) into 236LP/Super or into 214HK vs. standing; Run~Stop cancel: -8/-12 (OD: -5/-9)

kimberly-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
kimberly-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Most important normal in footsies, particularly canceled into Drive Rush; Run~Stop cancel: -1/-6 (OD: +2/-3)

kimberly-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
kimberly-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Whiffs vs. crouching opponents if not point blank unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; good combo and pressure starter; upper body is Air Invuln 10-15f (decent anti-air, but a little slow and cannot hit cross-up); the small hitbox on Kimberly's foot can only hit airborne opponents

kimberly-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
kimberly-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; important combo tool; Run~Stop cancel: -1/-9 (OD: +2/-6); special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame; extends a hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery (feet 6-23f, arm 6-15f with partial retraction 16-23f)

kimberly-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position
kimberly-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Forces stand on hit; good anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; in Drive Rush juggles, opponent is kept high enough to connect instant j.236PP for a ground bounce; can be up to +8/+3 meaty; best button for Run pressure (cancel into Arc Step beats 4f light normals); Run~Stop cancel: +3/-2 (OD: +6/+1)

kimberly-crouch-light-kick.name: Crouching Light Kick
kimberly-crouch-light-kick.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; slightly more pushback than other combo-starting lights, but can still chain 3 lights if starting point blank; can link to 5MP/2MP on Counter-hit or into 2HP out of close range Drive rush

kimberly-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
kimberly-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Non-cancelable, but can combo afterward and is a great option out of Drive Rush

kimberly-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: Knockdown time extended when attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

kimberly-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
kimberly-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; lower body projectile invuln 6-28f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

kimberly-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
kimberly-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; best hitbox for opponents directly below Kimberly; useful in juggles with 2 spraycans as it does not increase the opponent's juggle count; lower body projectile invuln 8-30f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

kimberly-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties

kimberly-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties
kimberly-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-30f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

kimberly-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
kimberly-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead (vs. Blanka, will not work after perfect safe jump j.HP/j.HK timing, making it useless in corner spraycan setup)

water-slicer-slide.name: Water Slicer Slide
notes: Slide along the ground toward your opponent. This is a low attack that cannot be blocked standing
water-slicer-slide.notes_sc: Low profile 9-21f (combined with upper body projectile invuln, great at avoiding projectiles); the large extended hurtbox above her leg is also projectile invuln (more susceptible to mid counter-pokes in neutral); if it connects in a free juggle, puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; the first 4 active frames have a set recovery that locks the frame advantage to +1/-5, and also cause a knockdown on Punish Counter (does not apply to later active frames, allowing combo extensions after punishing projectiles)

windmill-kick.name: Windmill Kick
notes: An overhead attack that cannot be blocked while crouching
Forces opponent to crouch on hit
windmill-kick.notes_sc: Forces crouch on hit, making opponents wider for easier follow-up links; causes spike KD vs. airborne opponents; allows link to 2LP naturally, and 5MP link out of Drive Rush (DR momentum does not allow 2MP, 5MP or 2HP to reach opponent)

hisen-kick.name: Hisen Kick
notes: A leaping kick with long reach. Useful as a counter to an opponent's sweeps
Considered airborne from frames 18 - 32
Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
Recovery is reduced by 8 frames when blocked
hisen-kick.notes_sc: Can hop over low pokes for a Punish Counter combo; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; can cancel into forward, neutral, or back jump on hit or block; 8f less recovery on block only; 1f more hitstun vs. crouching opponents; much shorter range on the final active frame

step-up.name: Step Up
notes: After hisen kick
Performed from Hisen Kick, this maneuver can be used to jump in one of three directions

elbow-drop.name: Elbow Drop
notes: At the peak of a forward jump
A descending attack with a long duration that can be performed while jumping
Forces a slam knockdown when hitting a mid-air opponent
elbow-drop.notes_sc: Can only be performed from Forward Jump; spike KD vs. airborne opponents; stops Kimberly's air momentum (useful for baiting anti-airs); maximum advantage is only possible when input at the latest possible timing (frame 30 of jump; otherwise, connects with 2nd to last active frame); minimum advantage is more likely to apply to tall standing characters, but is usually at least '''+5'''/-2

bushin-tiger-fangs.name: Bushin Tiger Fangs
notes: A quick series of attacks that can be canceled into special moves
bushin-tiger-fangs.notes_sc: Puts opponent into a limited juggle state; will not combo into 236HP; Run~Stop cancel: KD+34/-6 (OD: KD+37/-3)

bushin-prism-strikes-2.name: Bushin Prism Strikes (2)
notes: A quick combination attack that hits multiple times

bushin-prism-strikes-3.name: Bushin Prism Strikes (3)
notes: A quick combination attack that hits multiple times

bushin-prism-strikes-4.name: Bushin Prism Strikes (4)
notes: A quick combination attack that hits multiple times

bushin-hellchain-3.name: Bushin Hellchain (3)
notes: A combination attack that sends an opponent flying

bushin-hellchain-4.name: Bushin Hellchain (4)
notes: A combination attack that sends an opponent flying

bushin-hellchain-throw.name: Bushin Hellchain Throw
notes: A combination attack that switches places with your opponent
bushin-hellchain-throw.notes_sc: () refers to scaled damage from 5LP combo starter; throws opponent backwards for a side switch; very useful when Kimberly is cornered; cannot hit airborne opponents; true blockstring even at max delay; leads to a 9f safejab setup with immediate DR~2LP

bushin-senpukyaku-light.name: Bushin Senpu-kyaku (Light)
notes: Unleash a series of spinning kicks while leaping into the air. Useful in combos and as an anti-air attack
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 5 - 11
Considered airborne from frames 7 - 42
Invincible to throws from frames 7 - 11

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 5 - 11

bushin-senpukyaku-medium.name: Bushin Senpu-kyaku (Medium)
notes: Unleash a series of spinning kicks while leaping into the air. Useful in combos and as an anti-air attack
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 6 - 12
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 45
Invincible to throws from frames 8 - 12

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 6 - 12

bushin-senpukyaku-heavy.name: Bushin Senpu-kyaku (Heavy)
notes: Unleash a series of spinning kicks while leaping into the air. Useful in combos and as an anti-air attack
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 7 - 13
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 49
Invincible to throws from frames 9 - 13

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 7 - 13

bushin-senpukyaku-od.name: Bushin Senpu-kyaku (OD)
notes: Unleash a series of spinning kicks while leaping into the air. Useful in combos and as an anti-air attack
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 11
Considered airborne from frames 7 - 47
Invincible to throws from frames 7 - 11
Only the 4th hit can be canceled into a Level 2 Super Art

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 11

aerial-Bushin-senpukyaku.name: Aerial Bushin Senpu-kyaku
notes: A Bushin Senpukyaku performed while jumping. Effective when used to combo opponents being juggled in the air

aerial-Bushin-senpukyaku-od.name: Aerial Bushin Senpu-kyaku (OD)
notes: A Bushin Senpukyaku performed while jumping. Effective when used to combo opponents being juggled in the air
Has cross-up attack properties

sprint.name: Sprint
notes: Swiftly dash forward to close the distance with your opponent, during which a variety of moves can be performed
Kimberly can transition to branching attacks from the 8th frame
After the 16th frame, if the opponent is within a certain distance, Kimberly will automatically perform Arc Step
sprint.notes_sc: Holding forward causes Kimberly to continue running without activating Arc Step (must not let go of forward input at any point); cancels into follow-ups after 8 frames minimum (can cancel into Emergency Stop 2f earlier); Movement speed: 0.135/frame during cancelable frames

kimberly-emergency-stop.name: Emergency Stop
notes: During Sprint
Halt Sprint's movement to adjust the distance you cover or as a way to reduce how much you leave yourself open following a Sprint
kimberly-emergency-stop.notes_sc: Latest possible stop occurs after 41f (55f total animation); shortest Run~Stop distance: 1.64

torso-cleaver.name: Torso Cleaver
notes: During Sprint
A somersault kick performed from Sprint. Effective as an anti-air attack targeting opponents who tend to jump around a lot
torso-cleaver.notes_sc: Kicking legs are Anti-Air Invuln 18-27f (cannot hit cross-up); active frames 3-7 only hit airborne opponents above Kimberly (only active frames 1-2 are useful for meaty pressure); puts opponent into limited juggle state; () refers to higher KD advantage when juggled into

shadow-slide.name: Shadow Slide
notes: During Sprint
A sliding attack aimed at an opponent's feet, performed from Sprint. It is a low attack that cannot be blocked standing, and is effective when used as a surprise attack from a distance
shadow-slide.notes_sc: 6-21f low profile and upper body projectile invuln (only on the Slide itself); puts opponent into limited juggle state; advantage varies significantly depending on which active frame connects; () refers to KD advantage when juggled into (KD +47 from 5MP~HP unless delayed); cancel to SA3 can whiff when used from longer range

neck-hunter.name: Neck Hunter
notes: During Sprint
An overhead swinging kick performed from Sprint. It cannot be blocked crouching, and is useful as a way to break through an opponent's guard
neck-hunter.notes_sc: SA3 cancel can only combo in high connect juggles, or if overhead is absorbed by armor

arc-step.name: Arc Step
notes: Automatically activates after getting close with Sprint
Approach your opponent with Sprint and then kick off their body to leap backwards. Can be followed up with either an attack or a throw after the leap
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 43
Can transition into branching attacks from frame 35
arc-step.notes_sc: True blockstring when canceled from 2HP; can frame trap vs. 4f normals when canceled from 5HP, 5MP, 5MK, or 5MP~HP; only comes out if there is an opponent at close range on or near the ground after the 13th frame of OD Sprint; if opponent becomes airborne after startup, the whiffed attack has 1f active + 25f recovery; if Perfect Parried, Kimberly becomes airborne before opponent can Throw punish (can be punished with Drive Impact airborne connect or a Forced Knockdown normal up to 6f startup)

bushin-izuna-otoshi.name: Bushin Izuna Otoshi
notes: During Arc Step
A throw attack performed from Arc Step in which you leap onto and grab your opponent. Useful against opponents who keep their guard up after blocking Arc Step
With Classic Controls
Does 1656 individual damage when the attack lands as a Punish Counter
With Modern Controls
Does 1324 individual damage when the attack lands as a Punish Counter
bushin-izuna-otoshi.notes_sc: Throw connects against grounded opponents, whiffs vs. airborne or backdash; opponent is put into a limited juggle OTG state (slightly less time for juggle follow-ups than j.236PP)

bushin-hojin-kick.name: Bushin Hojin Kick
notes: During Arc Step
A move performed from Arc Step in which you descend diagonally with a swift kick. Useful in combos and as a way to lock down opponents who try to move after Arc Step
bushin-hojin-kick.notes_sc: Causes Wall Splat on hit near the corner; 2nd hit of the divekick occurs on hit only (1 hit on whiff or block); SA2 cancel works on either hit

sprint-od.name: Sprint (OD)
notes: Swiftly dash forward to close the distance with your opponent, during which a variety of moves can be performed
Kimberly can transition to Emergency Stop from the 6th frame
Kimberly can transition to Torso Cleaver, Shadow Slide and Neck Hunter from the 8th frame
After the 14th frame, if the opponent is within a certain distance, Kimberly will automatically perform Arc Step

kimberly-emergency-stop-od.name: Emergency Stop (OD)
notes: During Sprint OD
Halt Sprint's movement to adjust the distance you cover or as a way to reduce how much you leave yourself open following a Sprint

torso-cleaver-od.name: Torso Cleaver (OD)
notes: During Sprint OD
A somersault kick performed from Sprint. Effective as an anti-air attack targeting opponents who tend to jump around a lot

shadow-slide-od.name: Shadow Slide (OD)
notes: During Sprint OD
A sliding attack aimed at an opponent's feet, performed from Sprint. It is a low attack that cannot be blocked standing, and is effective when used as a surprise attack from a distance

neck-hunter-od.name: Neck Hunter (OD)
notes: During Sprint OD
An overhead swinging kick performed from Sprint. It cannot be blocked crouching, and is useful as a way to break through an opponent's guard
Adds a floor bounding effect during a mid-air hit

arc-step-od.name: Arc Step (OD)
notes: During Sprint OD
Approach your opponent with Sprint and then kick off their body to leap backwards. Can be followed up with either an attack or a throw after the leap
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 43
Can transition into branching attacks from frame 35

bushin-izuna-otoshi-od.name: Bushin Izuna Otoshi (OD)
notes: During Arc Step OD
A throw attack performed from Arc Step in which you leap onto and grab your opponent. Useful against opponents who keep their guard up after blocking Arc Step
With Classic Controls
Does 1035 individual damage when the attack lands as a Punish Counter
With Modern Controls
Does 828 individual damage when the attack lands as a Punish Counter

bushin-hojin-kick-od.name: Bushin Hojin Kick (OD)
notes: During Arc Step OD
A move performed from Arc Step in which you descend diagonally with a swift kick. Useful in combos and as a way to lock down opponents who try to move after Arc Step
Only transitions to the 2nd attack on hit
Produces a wall splat on hit

vagabond-edge-light.name: Vagabond Edge (Light)
notes: Lunge forward while unleashing a powerful chop. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos. The heavy and Overdrive versions can be canceled with a jump

vagabond-edge-medium.name: Vagabond Edge (Medium)
notes: Lunge forward while unleashing a powerful chop. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos. The heavy and Overdrive versions can be canceled with a jump
Will only transition to 2nd attack if the first attack hits

vagabond-edge-heavy.name: Vagabond Edge (Heavy)
notes: Lunge forward while unleashing a powerful chop. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos. The heavy and Overdrive versions can be canceled with a jump
Will only transition to 2nd attack if the first attack hits
The 2nd attack can be canceled by a jump

vagabond-edge-od.name: Vagabond Edge (OD)
notes: Lunge forward while unleashing a powerful chop. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos. The heavy and Overdrive versions can be canceled with a jump
Will only transition to 2nd attack if the first attack hits
The 2nd attack can be canceled by a jump

hidden-variable.name: Hidden Variable
notes: Conceal yourself with smoke and instantly appear before your opponent. Useful as a way to close the distance with your opponent or as a way to launch a surprise offensive
Completely invincible from frames 19 - 26
Can be canceled into anything except walk/dashes, Drive Parry, Block and Hidden Variable from the 34th frame
hidden-variable.notes_sc: After reappearing, can act after 7f recovery; air follow-ups have the same properties as regular jump attacks (including 3 landing recovery frames); if Kimberly does not act, she has 1 punishable landing recovery frame, then 2 landing frames where she can block or cancel into an attack; cannot use j.236P after reappearing mid-air

hidden-variable-od.name: Hidden Variable (OD)
notes: Conceal yourself with smoke and instantly appear before your opponent. Useful as a way to close the distance with your opponent or as a way to launch a surprise offensive
Completely invincible from frames 20 - 27
Can be canceled into a mid-air attack from the 34th frame
Landing recovery can be canceled by anything except walk/dashes

genius-at-play.name: Genius at Play
notes: Tamper with a spray can to get an additional stock. Once stocked, input the same command again to activate a Shuriken Bomb
Adds a Shuriken Bomb stock on frame 43
genius-at-play.notes_sc: Refills 2 spray cans; cannot reload if a spray can is currently stocked or is active on-screen

shuriken-bomb-light.name: Shuriken Bomb (Light)
notes: With at least 1 shuriken bomb stock
Toss out and set a spray can that will explode after a period of time. Best used to apply pressure that capitalizes on the duration between when the bomb is placed and its detonation
Fires a projectile on the 18th frame
Decreases the Shuriken Bomb stock by 1
Values represent when the attack is performed in the middle of the screen

shuriken-bomb-medium.name: Shuriken Bomb (Medium)
notes: With at least 1 shuriken bomb stock
Toss out and set two spray cans that will explode after a period of time. Can be used following an OD Genius at Play
Fires a projectile on the 18th frame
Decreases the Shuriken Bomb stock by 1
Values represent when the attack is performed in the middle of the screen

shuriken-bomb-heavy.name: Shuriken Bomb (Heavy)
notes: With at least 1 shuriken bomb stock
Toss out and set two spray cans that will explode after a period of time. Can be used following an OD Genius at Play
Fires a projectile on the 18th frame
Decreases the Shuriken Bomb stock by 1
Values represent when the attack is performed in the middle of the screen

genius-at-play-od.name: Genius at Play (OD)
notes: Tamper with a spray can to get an additional stock. Once stocked, input the same command again to activate a Shuriken Bomb
Adds 2 Shuriken Bomb stock on frame 43

shuriken-bomb-spread-light.name: Shuriken Bomb Spread (Light)
notes: With at least 2 shuriken bomb stocks
Toss out and set two spray cans that will explode after a period of time. Can be used following an OD Genius at Play
Fires 2 projectiles on the 18th frame (Light and Medium versions)
Decreases the Shuriken Bomb stock by 2
Projectile properties are the same as the individual attacks

shuriken-bomb-spread-medium.name: Shuriken Bomb Spread (Medium)
notes: With at least 2 shuriken bomb stocks
Toss out and set two spray cans that will explode after a period of time. Can be used following an OD Genius at Play
Fires 2 projectiles on the 18th frame (Light and Heavy versions)
Decreases the Shuriken Bomb stock by 2
Projectile properties are the same as the individual attacks

shuriken-bomb-spread-heavy.name: Shuriken Bomb Spread (Heavy)
notes: With at least 2 shuriken bomb stocks
Toss out and set two spray cans that will explode after a period of time. Can be used following an OD Genius at Play
Fires 2 projectiles on the 18th frame (Medium and Heavy versions)
Decreases the Shuriken Bomb stock by 2
Projectile properties are the same as the individual attacks

nue-twister.name: Nue Twister
notes: Grab an airborne opponent and smash them into the ground below. Its high damage makes it an effective anti-air or mid-air combo option
Does 1656 individual damage when the attack lands as a Punish Counter
nue-twister.notes_sc: Air throw that can be comboed into; causes OTG bounce (limited juggle state); no minimum height requirement; the first 15 landing recovery frames put Kimberly in a crouching state; Range: 1.0

nue-twister-od.name: Nue Twister (OD)
notes: Grab an airborne opponent and smash them into the ground below. Its high damage makes it an effective anti-air or mid-air combo option
Does 1035 individual damage when the attack lands as a Punish Counter

bushin-beats.name: Bushin Beats
notes: Rush forward and knock down an opponent, then follow up with a flurry of attacks. Use this as a part of a combo while on the ground, or as a follow-up attack if they're airborne
Invincible to strikes and throws on frames 1 - 14
Minimum guaranteed damage is 30%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws on frames 1 - 14

bushin-beats.notes_sc: Travels ~75% screen length; does not throw spraycan without holding the button

bushin-thunderous-beats.name: Bushin Thunderous Beats
notes: With at least 1 shuriken bomb stock
Hold to use shuriken
Adds a Shuriken Bomb follow-up attack after using Bushin Beats. Damage increases but requires one stock of Shuriken Bombs to activate
Invincible to strikes and throws on frames 1 - 14
Minimum guaranteed damage is 30%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws on frames 1 - 14

bushin-thunderous-beats.notes_sc: Holding the button consumes 1 can for better damage and oki; travels ~75% screen length; spraycan only does 1 Drive damage to prevent opponent from recovering Drive meter too quickly

bushin-scramble.name: Bushin Scramble
notes: Ensnare the opponent with a dive bomb-like attack from the air, and then pelt them relentlessly with mid-air attacks. An excellent choice against projectiles or airborne opponents
Completely invincible on the 1st frame
Considered airborne from frames 1- 20
Minimum guaranteed damage is 40%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
bushin-scramble.notes_sc: Becomes airborne during screen freeze (can dodge grounded attacks despite not having invincibility); screen freeze causes active spraycans to disappear; first hit before cinematic does 300 damage

soaring-bushin-scramble.name: Soaring Bushin Scramble
notes: Activate Bushin Scramble in mid-air to dive bomb the opponent and attack them relentlessly. Easy to use in a combo from a mid-air special move
Completely invincible on the 1st frame
Considered airborne from frame 1 and on
Minimum guaranteed damage is 40%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
soaring-bushin-scramble.notes_sc: Can only be used from Forward Jump; screen freeze causes active spraycans to disappear; first hit before cinematic does 300 damage; 2f startup when canceled from OD special moves only

bushin-ninjastar-cypher.name: Bushin Ninjastar Cypher
notes: Launch the opponent into the air, and attack them while detonating numerous spray cans. After the attack, Kimberly's walk speed and attack power will be boosted
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 13
On the first frame, the following boosts are activated and remain until the end of the match
- Attack damage up 111%
- Walk speed increased
Minimum guaranteed damage is 50%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Can only be canceled into from special moves
bushin-ninjastar-cypher.notes_sc: Immediately upon activation, Kimberly gains 11% buff to attack damage and walk speed for the rest of the match; cinematic time regenerates ~1.9 Drive bars for Kimberly

bushin-ninjastar-cypher-ca.name: Bushin Ninjastar Cypher (Critical Art)
notes: When under 25% vitality
Launch the opponent into the air, and attack them while detonating numerous spray cans. After the attack, Kimberly's walk speed and attack power will be boosted
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 13
On the first frame, the following boosts are activated and remain until the end of the match
- Attack damage up 111%
- Walk speed increased
Minimum guaranteed damage is 50%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Can only be canceled into from special moves

ripcord-throw.name: Ripcord Throw
notes: When near opponent
Break your opponent's poise with a chop, leap over them to disrupt their balance, and toss them aside. Throws your opponent without switching places with them
Punish Counter properties
- Damage set to 1838 (1839)
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Produces a Hard Knockdown
ripcord-throw.notes_sc: Can walk forward for a strong manually-timed corner throw loop; starter scaling applies to Spraycan juggle follow-ups; damage distribution is 501,581 (PC: 700,1138) and only the first hit will apply damage when comboing into Spraycan; Punish Counter also splits Meter data into 2 equal hits (only deals +199 damage and 0.5 bars of Drive Damage on spraycan setups vs. Parry); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

bell-ringer.name: Bell Ringer
notes: When near opponent

Place both legs on your opponent's torso and perform a knee strike, then use the force of pulling them toward the ground to throw them behind you. Throws your opponent while switching places with them
Punish Counter properties
- Damage set to 1838 (1839)
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Produces a Hard Knockdown
bell-ringer.notes_sc: Side switches; no throw oki after back throwing opponent into corner, but can meaty with strikes; starter scaling applies to Spraycan juggle follow-ups (requires staggered 2x Spraycans, follow-up juggles very limited); damage distribution is 401,681 (PC: 451,1388) and only the first hit will apply damage when comboing into Spraycan; Punish Counter also splits Meter data into 2 equal hits (only deals +50 damage and 0.5 bars of Drive Damage on spraycan juggles); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously
